headline: Israel FLOOR CONFIRMATION at 0.0 / Critical — conduct documentation update for May 10 flotilla activist deportation. No score change (floor). Saif Abukeshek (Spanish-Swedish, Palestinian origin) and Thiago Avila (Brazilian) — both members of the Global Sumud Flotilla steering committee — deported May 10 after ~11 days detention; transferred to Athens. Israeli FM described them as 'professional provocateurs'; no charges filed despite Israel's initial 'terrorist affiliation' and 'illegal activity' framing. Deportation closes the arbitrary-detention phase of the May 8 OHCHR-documented conduct event but DOES NOT resolve the broader conduct categories: (1) OHCHR torture/inhumane-treatment allegations involving 35 individuals remain unaddressed (separate from deportation duration); (2) UNCLOS jurisdiction question (interception at 500 nautical miles, international waters) unaddressed; (3) UNCAT accountability questions remain open. Floor-conduct record updated: 'arbitrary detention duration resolved by deportation' — does NOT constitute 'accountability achieved.' Distinction matters for floor-exit criteria.

conduct_documentation.summary: Day 11 of the flotilla detention event resolved by deportation on May 10. The deportation closes one open conduct category (arbitrary-detention duration) but leaves three open: (1) torture/inhumane-treatment allegations during detention; (2) UNCLOS jurisdiction questions over international-waters interception; (3) UNCAT accountability for the 35-individual mistreatment cluster.
conduct_documentation.may_10_conduct_event: Saif Abu Keshek and Thiago Ávila deported to Athens after ~11 days Israeli detention. Israeli FM: 'professional provocateurs.' Initial Israeli detention framing alleged 'terrorist affiliation' and 'illegal activity' — no charges filed at deportation. Abu Keshek publicly stated he would continue protesting upon arrival in Athens.
open_conduct_categories_post_deportation: OHCHR May 8 torture/inhumane-treatment allegations involving 35 individuals — separate from detention duration; unaddressed and unresolved.
UNCLOS jurisdiction questions: April 29 interception at 500 nautical miles (international waters) — jurisdictional accountability question unanswered.
UNCAT accountability for reported mistreatment during detention — no investigation announced, no remedy provided.

Distinction: 'resolved by deportation' ≠ 'accountability achieved' — the deportation removes the detainees but does not constitute remedy, investigation, or acknowledgment of the underlying violation.

structural_floor_record_updates.closed_category: April 29 – May 10 arbitrary-detention duration event (Abu Keshek, Avila): resolved by deportation
remains_open_categories: Gaza military operations conduct (primary floor anchor)
International-waters humanitarian-interdiction with torture allegations (added May 9)
Post-deportation accountability gap: torture allegations + UNCLOS + UNCAT — not addressed by deportation

conduct_documentation.floor_exit_criteria_impact: Deportation event does not move Israel toward floor exit. The closure of one detention-duration category against three unresolved-violation categories sustains the negative-conduct trajectory. Floor-exit criteria would require Israel to acknowledge, investigate, or provide remedy for the torture allegations — none of which occurred in window.

key_evidence: May 10 2026 (in-window): Saif Abu Keshek (Spanish-Swedish) and Thiago Ávila (Brazilian) deported from Israel after ~11 days detention; transferred to Athens.
May 10 2026 (in-window): Israeli FM described both as 'professional provocateurs'; no charges filed despite initial Israeli allegations of 'terrorist affiliation' and 'illegal activity.'
May 10 2026 (in-window): Abu Keshek publicly stated he would continue protesting upon arrival in Athens — detainee-side accountability narrative preserved.
May 8, 2026 (carry-over): OHCHR press release documented torture/inhumane-treatment allegations involving 35 individuals during detention period — separate from detention duration.

April 29, 2026 (carry-over): Maritime interception at 500 nautical miles (international waters) — UNCLOS jurisdiction question opened; 22 boats intercepted, 180 civilians detained, two transferred to Israel (the now-deported pair).
Post-deportation: torture allegations, UNCLOS jurisdiction, UNCAT accountability all remain unaddressed by deportation event.

evidence.source: NBC News — Israel deports activists aid flotilla Gaza
evidence.finding: Deportation event May 10; deportation closes arbitrary-detention duration; torture allegations remain unaddressed.

evidence.source: Washington Post — Israel Gaza flotilla activists Sumud Avila
evidence.finding: Deportation event confirmed; Israeli FM framing of 'professional provocateurs'; no charges filed.

evidence.source: PBS Newshour — Two activists detained over leading an aid flotilla to Gaza are deported by Israel
evidence.finding: Multi-source confirmation; ~11 days detention period; no charges; deportation framing as resolution.

evidence.source: UPI — Deport two Global Sumud Flotilla activists Gaza
evidence.finding: Deportation event independently confirmed; Athens transfer documented; detainee statements about continued advocacy.

evidence.source: Al Jazeera — Israel deports two abducted Gaza aid flotilla activists
evidence.finding: Deportation event with 'abducted' framing — preserves the rights-organization characterization of interception as unlawful seizure; torture allegations referenced.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 1 unchanged (floor). State suppresses independent monitoring of detainee conditions; OHCHR access remains constrained.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 1 unchanged (floor). Detention-phase mistreatment allegations remain unresolved; deportation does not constitute empathic response to alleged harm.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 1 unchanged (floor). Deportation is a release-from-detention action but not an accountability action; no remedy provided for alleged mistreatment.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 1 unchanged (floor). Continued floor-status conduct on civilian-protection norms in Gaza operations + humanitarian-interdiction conduct category.
scoring_rationale.BND: 1 unchanged (floor). UNCLOS jurisdiction question unanswered; B3 scope-clarity floor-active.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 1 unchanged (floor). No accountability action announced for torture allegations; AB1 harm-acknowledgment floor active; AB4 accountability-to-external-norms floor active.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 1 unchanged (floor). No structural-reform signals; floor sustained.
scoring_rationale.INT: 1 unchanged (floor). Deportation without charges contradicts initial 'terrorist affiliation' framing — internal-consistency violation; INT floor active.

math_check.validator_check: Floor sustained. Composite cannot move below floor. Floor conduct record updated.

negativeSignals: Deportation without charges contradicts initial Israeli framing (terrorist affiliation; illegal activity); torture/inhumane-treatment allegations unaddressed; UNCLOS jurisdiction question unanswered; UNCAT accountability not pursued; 'professional provocateurs' framing minimizes humanitarian-actor identity; continuing Gaza operations conduct.
positiveSignals: Deportation event itself closes the arbitrary-detention duration component — detainees released from Israeli custody. Insufficient to move floor: closure of one category against three unresolved-violation categories.
sector_context: Floor cluster. Israel's floor record now contains: (1) Gaza military operations conduct (primary anchor); (2) international-waters humanitarian-interdiction conduct category (added May 9); (3) torture allegations and UNCLOS/UNCAT accountability gaps (added May 9, sustained May 10 by deportation-without-accountability framing). The dual-conduct-category accumulation continues to entrench the floor placement.
open_actions_resolved: Day 11 detention duration closed by May 10 deportation
Detainee names and details documented in floor record
Israeli FM 'professional provocateurs' framing recorded for accountability tracking
open_actions_carried: Watch: any Israeli response to OHCHR May 8 torture allegations (35-individual cluster)
Watch: UNCLOS jurisdiction follow-up by Spanish, Swedish, Brazilian governments
Watch: Gaza operations conduct (primary floor anchor)
methodology_consistency_check: Day-2 conduct update applies the same floor-protocol rules used on May 9: floor entities receive conduct documentation without composite movement; closure of one violation category does not constitute floor-exit-criteria progress unless accountability is achieved. Confirmed methodologically consistent.
